levels.title: Identification & Form
levels.miniLesson:
🎯 Level 1: The 'Object-First' Rule

The passive voice is formed using be (in the correct tense) + past participle (V3). We use it when the action is more important than who did it.

TensePassive FormExample
Present Simpleam/is/are + V3The tea is grown in India.
Past Simplewas/were + V3The window was broken.
Futurewill be + V3The results will be sent.

questionBank.question: Which sentence is in the Passive Voice?
options: The meal was cooked by the chef.
The chef was cooking the meal.
questionBank.correctAnswer: The meal was cooked by the chef.
questionBank.explanation: It uses the 'be + past participle' structure where the subject (the meal) receives the action.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct past passive form: 'The letters ___ yesterday.'
questionBank.explanation: Plural subject 'letters' + past tense 'yesterday' requires 'were delivered'.

questionBank.question: Identify the passive verb in: 'Rice is grown in many Asian countries.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Is' (be) + 'grown' (past participle of grow).

questionBank.question: Complete the sentence: 'The bridge ___ in 1950.'
questionBank.explanation: Specific past date requires 'was' + the past participle 'built'.

questionBank.question: Which of these is the correct past participle for 'write'?
questionBank.explanation: The passive voice always uses the 3rd form (V3) of the verb.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The report was wrote by the manager.'
questionBank.explanation: Should be the past participle 'written'.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct present passive form: 'English ___ all over the world.'
questionBank.explanation: General fact requires present 'is' + V3 'spoken'.

questionBank.question: Which sentence is correct for a future action?
options: The room will be cleaned tomorrow.
The room will be clean tomorrow.
questionBank.correctAnswer: The room will be cleaned tomorrow.
questionBank.explanation: 'Will be' + past participle is the future passive.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'A lot of money was stole from the bank.'
questionBank.explanation: The past participle of 'steal' is 'stolen'.

questionBank.question: Complete: '___ the car repaired yet?'
questionBank.explanation: 'Is the car repaired' is the correct present passive question.

questionBank.question: Which is correct? 'The pyramids ___ by the ancient Egyptians.'
questionBank.explanation: Plural 'pyramids' + past tense.

questionBank.question: Identify the passive form: 'The secret was kept for years.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Was' (be) + 'kept' (V3 of keep).

questionBank.question: Choose the correct verb: 'This song ___ by millions of people.'
questionBank.explanation: Present passive 'is' + V3 'loved'.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The cake were eaten by the children.'
questionBank.explanation: Singular 'cake' requires 'was'.

questionBank.question: Complete the sentence: 'The results ___ next week.'
questionBank.explanation: Future passive.

questionBank.question: Which is correct? 'The movie ___ by Spielberg.'
questionBank.explanation: Past passive.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The windows is cleaned every week.'
questionBank.explanation: Plural 'windows' requires 'are'.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct form: 'The house ___ ten years ago.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Sold' is the V3 of 'sell'.

questionBank.question: Identify the passive construction: 'The email has been sent.'
questionBank.explanation: Present Perfect Passive: 'has been' + V3.

questionBank.question: Complete: 'Many books ___ into Japanese every year.'
questionBank.explanation: Present passive for plural 'books'.

questionBank.question: Which is correct? 'The package ___ by tomorrow.'
questionBank.explanation: Future passive.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The room was tidy up by the staff.'
questionBank.explanation: Should be the past participle 'tidied up'.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct form: 'Thousands of cars ___ in this factory.'
questionBank.explanation: Present passive for plural 'cars'.

questionBank.question: Identify the passive: 'I was given a gift.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Was' + V3 'given'.

questionBank.question: Complete the sentence: 'The truth ___ finally.'
questionBank.explanation: Past passive.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The light was turn off.'
questionBank.explanation: Should be 'turned'.

questionBank.question: Which is correct? 'His name ___ in the list.'
questionBank.explanation: Present passive.

questionBank.question: Complete: '___ the room cleaned yesterday?'
questionBank.explanation: Past passive question for singular 'room'.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The keys were found on the floor.'
questionBank.explanation: Correct past passive for plural 'keys'.

questionBank.question: Final Check: In a Passive sentence, the subject ___ the action.
questionBank.explanation: The passive voice shifts focus from the 'doer' to the 'receiver'.

levels.title: L1 Traps: Dropping the Agent
levels.miniLesson:
⚠️ Level 2: The 'Objectivity' Shift

In the passive voice, we only use 'by + agent' if the person is important or surprising. In most academic contexts, we drop the agent to keep the focus on the process or the result.

  • ❌ Redundant: The road was built by workers. (Obvious).
  • ✅ Objective: The road was built in 1990.
  • ❌ L1 Trap: The mistake was happened. (Error: 'Happen' is an intransitive verb and cannot be passive).
  • ✅ Correct: The mistake happened.

questionBank.question: Which sentence is the most appropriate for a formal report?
options: The data was collected by the researchers.
questionBank.explanation: In formal reporting, dropping the agent (the researchers) makes the statement more objective.

questionBank.question: Identify the 'Intransitive Verb' error: 'The accident was occurred at midnight.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Occur' is like 'happen'; it cannot be used in the passive voice. It should be 'The accident occurred'.

questionBank.question: When is it NECESSARY to use the 'by' phrase?
options: When the agent is obvious (e.g., the police).
When the agent is a specific, famous person (e.g., 'The play was written by Shakespeare').
When you want the sentence to be longer.
questionBank.correctAnswer: When the agent is a specific, famous person (e.g., 'The play was written by Shakespeare').
questionBank.explanation: We use 'by' when the specific identity of the doer is the most important information.

questionBank.question: Fix the redundancy: 'The crime was investigated by the police.'
options: Both are better than the original passive with 'by the police'.
questionBank.correctAnswer: Both are better than the original passive with 'by the police'.
questionBank.explanation: Since only police investigate crimes, 'by the police' is redundant in the passive.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The sun is risen in the east.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Rise' is an intransitive verb. It should be the active 'The sun rises'.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct form: 'The decision ___ yesterday.'
questionBank.explanation: Standard past passive without an unnecessary agent.

questionBank.question: Which verb CANNOT be used in the passive voice?
questionBank.explanation: 'Die' has no object; you cannot 'die something,' so it cannot be made passive.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The package was arrived this morning.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Arrive' is intransitive. Correct form: 'The package arrived'.

questionBank.question: Which sentence sounds more objective?
questionBank.explanation: The passive voice removes personal pronouns (I/We), creating a neutral tone.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The house was belonged to my grandfather.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Belong' is a state verb that cannot be passive. Correct: 'The house belonged to...'.

questionBank.question: Which 'by' phrase is helpful?
options: The window was broken by someone.
The email was sent by a computer.
The law was passed by a narrow margin of votes.
The cake was eaten by a person.
questionBank.correctAnswer: The law was passed by a narrow margin of votes.
questionBank.explanation: This 'by' phrase provides crucial, non-obvious information.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'He was disappeared into the woods.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Disappear' is intransitive. Correct: 'He disappeared'.

questionBank.question: Choose the best academic choice: 'The participants ___ into three groups.'
questionBank.explanation: Passive is needed to show the researchers' action on the participants.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The book is consisted of ten chapters.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Consist' is always active: 'The book consists of...'.

questionBank.question: Complete: 'Smoking ___ in the building.'
questionBank.explanation: The rule is the focus, not who made the rule.

questionBank.question: Which verb is transitive (can be passive)?
questionBank.explanation: You can 'destroy a city,' so 'The city was destroyed' is possible.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'I was graduated from university in 2020.'
questionBank.explanation: While technically possible in old English, modern English is always active: 'I graduated'.

questionBank.question: Which sentence avoids an unnecessary agent?
options: English is spoken in Australia by people.
questionBank.explanation: The fact that 'people' speak it is obvious.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'Many problems were resulted from the new policy.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Result' is intransitive. Correct: 'Many problems resulted from...'.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct form: 'The thief ___ by the end of the day.'
questionBank.explanation: Past passive using the V3 of 'catch'.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The sun is set behind the mountains.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Set' (for the sun) is intransitive. Correct: 'The sun sets'.

questionBank.question: Which is more appropriate for a news headline?
questionBank.explanation: The object (diamonds) is the focus.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The car was collided with a truck.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Collide' is intransitive. Correct: 'The car collided'.

questionBank.question: Complete: 'This computer ___ in Japan.'
questionBank.explanation: Passive focuses on the origin.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The story was seemed very interesting.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Seem' is a linking verb and cannot be passive.

questionBank.question: Which sentence has a useful 'by' phrase?
options: The dinner was made by my mom.
The dinner was made by using ingredients.
The dinner was made by a professional chef.
Both A and C are useful depending on the story.
questionBank.correctAnswer: Both A and C are useful depending on the story.
questionBank.explanation: Specific individuals add meaning.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The phone is belonged to her.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Belong' is never passive.

questionBank.question: Complete the phrase: 'The project ___ next week.'
questionBank.explanation: Future passive.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'It was happened suddenly.'
questionBank.explanation: Intransitive verb 'happen' cannot be passive.

questionBank.question: Final Check: Intransitive verbs (happen, exist, arrive) ___.
options: Take a passive form only in the past.
Take a passive form only in the present.
questionBank.explanation: Since they have no object, there is nothing to move to the 'subject' position.

levels.title: Contextual Correction
levels.miniLesson:
📝 Level 3: The Passive-Active Balance

Academic excellence requires knowing when to be passive. Use the passive to describe methodology, but use the active voice to describe the logical conclusions of the author.

  • 🔍 The Methodology Shift: The samples were collected (Passive) and then analyzed (Passive). The results suggest (Active) a new trend.
  • 📊 The Wordy Trap: A decision was made by the committee. (Heavy) ➔ The committee decided. (Punchy and clear).

questionBank.question: Identify the error in this paragraph: 'The experiment was designed by the team. Then, the data was collected and the results were appeared to be significant.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Appear' is an intransitive verb; it cannot be made passive. It should be 'the results appeared'.

questionBank.question: Which sentence is the best academic rewrite for: 'We saw the bacteria under the microscope.'?
options: The bacteria was seen under the microscope.
The bacteria were seen under the microscope.
The bacteria seen under the microscope.
questionBank.correctAnswer: The bacteria were seen under the microscope.
questionBank.explanation: 'Bacteria' is plural (singular is 'bacterium'), so it requires 'were'.

questionBank.question: Find the redundant agent: 'The research was conducted by scientists in a laboratory setting.'
questionBank.explanation: Since research is inherently done by scientists, this phrase adds no new information to a formal report.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct form for a recent change: 'New safety measures ___ by the city council.'
questionBank.explanation: Present Perfect Passive: 'have been' + V3 'implemented' matches the plural 'measures'.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The report has been writing by the intern for three days.'
questionBank.explanation: This should be the passive 'has been written' or the active 'the intern has been writing'.

questionBank.question: Which is correct? 'The artifacts ___ in an ancient tomb.'
questionBank.explanation: Plural subject 'artifacts' requires 'were'.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'The meeting was took place in the conference room.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Take place' is an intransitive idiom. Correct form: 'The meeting took place'.

questionBank.question: Choose the best academic choice: 'The theory ___ by many experts in the field.'
questionBank.explanation: Present passive showing the current status of the theory.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'He was told to not tell anyone about the plan.'
questionBank.explanation: Formal preference: 'not to tell' (avoiding the split infinitive after the passive 'was told').

questionBank.question: Which is correct? 'The data ___ for any errors.'
questionBank.explanation: In formal academic contexts, 'data' is the plural of 'datum' and takes 'were'.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'The suspect was remained in custody for further questioning.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Remain' is an intransitive verb and cannot be passive. Correct: 'The suspect remained'.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct form: 'Thousands of emails ___ every second.'
questionBank.explanation: Present passive for a general, ongoing fact.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The book was published in 2020 and it has been read by millions of people since then.'
questionBank.explanation: Correct use of past passive for the event and present perfect passive for the ongoing status.

questionBank.question: Which is correct? 'The building ___ by the end of the year.'
questionBank.explanation: Future passive structure.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'The information was found to be incorrect by the committee.'
questionBank.explanation: Grammatically sound; the 'by' phrase is useful as it identifies who made the judgment.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct form: 'The problem ___ by the engineers right now.'
questionBank.explanation: Present Continuous Passive: 'is being' + V3 'solved'.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The bridge was fell down during the earthquake.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Fall' is intransitive. It should be 'The bridge fell down'.

questionBank.question: Which is correct? 'The results ___ next Friday.'
questionBank.explanation: Future passive.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'The decision was reached after much discussion.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Reach a decision' is a transitive phrase; passive is correct.

questionBank.question: Choose the best academic form: 'It ___ that the project will succeed.'
questionBank.explanation: Standard impersonal passive 'It is believed that...' used for objectivity.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The car was belonged to a collector.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Belong' is always active.

questionBank.question: Which is correct? 'The package ___ to your office.'
questionBank.explanation: Present perfect passive.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'The fire was spreading quickly through the building.'
questionBank.explanation: Active past continuous is correct here.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct form: 'The students ___ about the changes yesterday.'
questionBank.explanation: Past passive.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The mountain is consisted of granite.'
questionBank.explanation: Should be active 'consists of'.

questionBank.question: Which is correct? 'The window ___ when I arrived.'
questionBank.explanation: Past Perfect Passive showing an action completed before another past event.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'The law was passed by a majority vote.'
questionBank.explanation: The 'by' phrase is necessary and informative here.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct form: 'The treasure ___ in a hidden cave.'
questionBank.explanation: Past passive V3.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The accident was happened at 5 PM.'
questionBank.explanation: Intransitive 'happen' cannot be passive.

questionBank.question: Final Check: 'The email ___ by the manager.'
questionBank.explanation: Standard past passive.

levels.title: IELTS/TOEFL Integration
levels.miniLesson:
📈 Level 4: The Process Specialist

In Task 1 Process Diagrams, the passive voice is mandatory because the 'agent' (the factory worker or the machine) is irrelevant. The focus must remain entirely on the stages of production.

  • ⚙️ The Process Chain: First, the raw materials are collected. Subsequently, they are transported to the factory where they are processed into the final product.
  • 🧠 Impersonal Passives (Task 2): It is often argued that... / It has been suggested that... (This adds a layer of academic distance).
  • 🛑 The Modal Passive: Immediate action must be taken. (Modal + be + V3).

questionBank.question: Choose the best opening for a Task 1 Process Diagram (e.g., Making Brick):
options: Workers dig the clay out of the ground.
The clay is dug out of the ground.
They are digging the clay from the ground.
Clay digs out of the ground.
questionBank.correctAnswer: The clay is dug out of the ground.
questionBank.explanation: The passive voice 'is dug' focuses on the material, which is standard for process descriptions.

questionBank.question: Identify the correct Modal Passive for a Task 2 conclusion:
options: The government should be fixed the problem.
The problem should be fixed by the government.
questionBank.correctAnswer: The problem should be fixed by the government.
questionBank.explanation: Modal (should) + be + V3 (fixed) is the correct passive structure for recommendations.

questionBank.question: Which sentence uses an 'Impersonal Passive' to introduce an opinion?
options: I think that technology is good.
It is widely believed that technology is beneficial.
questionBank.correctAnswer: It is widely believed that technology is beneficial.
questionBank.explanation: 'It is [V3] that...' is the hallmark of high-level, objective academic writing.

questionBank.question: Complete the process step: 'Once the glass has been collected, it ___ into small pieces.'
questionBank.explanation: Processes that happen 'always' or 'regularly' use the Present Simple Passive.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The data suggests that more research needs to be conduct.'
questionBank.explanation: After 'to be,' you must use the past participle 'conducted'.

questionBank.question: Which is the most formal way to describe a result? 'The study ___ in the journal last month.'
questionBank.explanation: Specific past time ('last month') requires the Past Simple Passive.

questionBank.question: Find the error: 'After the fruit is picked, it is then wash and dried.'
questionBank.explanation: Parallelism in the passive: both verbs must be V3 ('washed and dried').

questionBank.question: Choose the best passive for a 'Future Plan' (Task 2): 'The new law ___ next year.'
questionBank.explanation: Future passive for a planned event.

questionBank.question: Identify the impersonal passive: '___ that the climate is changing.'
questionBank.explanation: Impersonal passive structure for widely held facts.

questionBank.question: Which is correct for a 'Recent Trend'? 'Many new schools ___ in the area recently.'
questionBank.explanation: 'Recently' triggers the Present Perfect Passive ('have been' + V3).

questionBank.question: Final Check: Why is the passive voice preferred in Process Diagrams?
options: It makes the word count higher.
It removes the focus from the worker and places it on the product.
It is easier to write than the active voice.
It is only used for verbs ending in -ing.
questionBank.correctAnswer: It removes the focus from the worker and places it on the product.
questionBank.explanation: Objective reporting is the primary goal of Task 1 process descriptions.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The mixture is heated until it will be boiled.'
questionBank.explanation: In time clauses, we use the present: 'until it boils' (active) or 'is boiled' (passive).

questionBank.question: Which is correct? 'These results ___ with caution.'
questionBank.explanation: Modal passive: should + be + V3.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct form: 'English ___ by over a billion people.'
questionBank.explanation: Present passive for a general fact.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The building was design by a famous architect.'
questionBank.explanation: Needs the V3 form 'designed'.

questionBank.question: Which is correct for 'Ongoing Action'? 'A new stadium ___ in the city center.'
questionBank.explanation: Present Continuous Passive showing an action currently in progress.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The package has being delivered already.'
questionBank.explanation: Should be 'has been' (Present Perfect Passive).

questionBank.question: Choose the correct form: 'The windows ___ once a month.'
questionBank.explanation: Present passive for a regular routine.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'He was given the information by the secretary.'
questionBank.explanation: Correct passive with an 'agent' (secretary) that provides useful context.

questionBank.question: Which is best for Task 1? 'Finally, the finished goods ___ to retailers.'
questionBank.explanation: Plural subject 'goods' + Present Passive.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The decision will being made tomorrow.'
questionBank.explanation: Future passive is 'will be' + V3.

questionBank.question: Which is correct? 'The car ___ in 2020.'
questionBank.explanation: Past passive.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The flowers are watered by the gardener every day.'
questionBank.explanation: Grammatically correct; the 'by' phrase is optional but acceptable here.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct form: 'Many houses ___ in the storm.'
questionBank.explanation: Past passive for plural 'houses'.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The report is wrote by me.'
questionBank.explanation: Should be 'written'.

questionBank.question: Which is correct? 'The secret ___ for decades.'
questionBank.explanation: Present Perfect Passive for a state continuing from the past.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The bridge was build in three years.'
questionBank.explanation: Needs V3 'built'.

questionBank.question: Choose the correct form: 'The results ___ after the test.'
questionBank.explanation: Present passive for plural 'results'.

questionBank.question: Identify the error: 'The email was send this morning.'
questionBank.explanation: Needs V3 'sent'.

questionBank.question: Final Rule: The passive voice is a tool to make your writing ___.
questionBank.explanation: Shifting the focus to the action or object creates a neutral, academic tone.
